description: Add TmuxConfig to config and TmuxSession implementation of AgentSession trait, register tmux backend in registry
steps: 1. In src/config/mod.rs, add TmuxConfig struct with fields: session (String), auto_start (bool, default true), auto_restart (bool, default true). Use serde defaults for the booleans.

2. In src/config/mod.rs, add `pub tmux: Option<TmuxConfig>` field to the AgentEntry struct. Also add a validation rule in validate_agents(): if entry.backend == 'tmux' && entry.tmux.is_none(), bail with error similar to acp validation.
3. Create src/agent/tmux/mod.rs with: pub mod session; pub use session::{TmuxAgent, TmuxSession};
4. In src/agent/mod.rs, add `pub mod tmux;` after the existing `pub mod acp;` line.

5. Create src/agent/tmux/session.rs implementing TmuxSession struct that implements AgentSession trait. Key design: (a) TmuxSession holds: session_name (String), events_rx (mpsc::Receiver<AgentEvent>), event_tx (mpsc::Sender<AgentEvent>), alive (Arc<AtomicBool>), last_output (Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>) to track previously seen lines, poll_handle (tokio::task::JoinHandle for the background capture loop). (b) send() executes `tmux send-keys -t <session> -- <escaped_text> Enter` using tokio::process::Command. Text must be escaped for tmux (replace single quotes, backslashes, semicolons). (c) send_with_attachments() just calls send() with the prompt text (tmux cannot send binary attachments). (d) A background polling task runs every 150ms calling `tmux capture-pane -t <session> -p -S -100`, diffs output against last_output to find new lines, and emits AgentEvent::Text for new content. Detect Claude Code result state (prompt waiting pattern like a line ending with '>' or '$' after content) to emit AgentEvent::Result. Detect permission patterns (lines containing 'Allow' and tool permission prompts) to emit AgentEvent::PermissionRequest. (e) respond_permission() sends 'y' Enter or 'n' Enter via tmux send-keys. (f) alive() checks `tmux has-session -t <session>` exit code. (g) close() sends '/exit' Enter or C-c via tmux send-keys. (h) permission_responder() returns Arc<TmuxPermissionResponder> that also uses send-keys. (i) session_id() returns None (tmux sessions don't have agentbridge session IDs). (j) take_events/replace_events/events/drain_stale_events follow the same pattern as AcpSession.

6. Create TmuxAgent factory struct with fields: work_dir (PathBuf), tmux_config (TmuxConfig), project_name (String). It has start_session() that: checks if tmux session exists (tmux has-session), if not and auto_start is true creates it with `tmux new-session -d -s <name> -c <work_dir>` then `tmux send-keys -t <name> 'claude' Enter`. Then spawns the background poll task, returns TmuxSession.

7. In src/agent/registry.rs, add a 'tmux' match arm that: extracts entry.tmux config (error if missing), creates TmuxAgent, calls start_session(), returns Box<dyn AgentSession>. Add `use super::tmux::TmuxAgent;` import. Update the error message for unknown backends to include 'tmux' in valid list.
8. Run cargo check to verify everything compiles.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check passes with no errors
cargo test passes (existing tests still work, new code compiles)
TmuxConfig struct exists with session, auto_start, auto_restart fields
TmuxSession implements all AgentSession trait methods
Validation rejects tmux backend without tmux config
